title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: Today’s signal is light but still relevant for AI governance: the strongest fresh item is a policy-oriented proposal on youth AI safety with an international standards angle. A second OpenAI statement clarifies the company’s policy advocacy posture, while the remaining update is primarily a distribution announcement with limited direct trust-and-safety significance.

items.title: OpenAI proposes global coordination on youth AI safety
items.why_it_matters: It pushes international safeguards and standards for protecting minors as AI systems become more widely used.

items.title: OpenAI outlines its AI policy and political advocacy approach
items.why_it_matters: It clarifies how the company represents its policy positions and frames its support for regulation and safety.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
